hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2684 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّه إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي ُّع َّطِّى  رَّإ ْسَّهُّ ن َّاسِي اً إ َّوْ ن َّان ِماً ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Saeed al-A'raj asked Imam {a.s} about a person in the state of ihram seeking 
shade from the sun using a stick or their hand.  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, except in the case of an illness."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2683

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2685 - وَّ ق ِى رِوَّإي َّه ِ جَّزِي ز ٍ
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi asked Imam {a.s} about a person in the state of ihram who covers 
their head unintentionally or while sleeping.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They should recite the Talbiyah when they remember."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2684

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Hariz: "They should remove the covering, recite the 
Talbiyah, and there is nothing upon them (no penalty).   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2685

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2687  -  وسَّا َّلَّ ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّق َّغُّ إَّلذ  ُّن َّاب ُّ عَّلَّى وَّج ْهِهِ جِي ن َّ ي ُّزِي ذُّ إَّلن  َّ وْمَّ ق َّي َّمْن َّعُّهُّ مِن َّ 
إَّلن  َّوْمِ إ َّ ي ُّع َّطِّى  وَّج ْهَّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن َّامَّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
He was asked about a person in the state of ihram sleeping on their face while 
on their mount.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in that."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 511  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2686

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2688 - وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم إ ِلَّى ن َّحْزِهَّا
hadiths.en:
Zurara asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about a person in the state of ihram who has flies 
landing on their face, preventing them from sleeping, and whether they may cover their 
face when they wish to sleep.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 308 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
597 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 506  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2689   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّحْن ُّوب ٍ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ رِن َّاب ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْ هِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ 
رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّل َّمَّ ط ُّق ُّزإً مِن ْ  إ َّط َّاق ِي زِةِ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّحْزِم  ق َّال ل َّهَّا  
ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّمُّ س َّاة ٍ ى  مَّح ْلِسٍ وَّإجِذٍ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّم  
وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق َّعَّلَّهُّ مُّي َّق َّزِّق اً ق ِى  مَّح ْلِسَّي ْن ِ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّمَّان ِ.
hadiths.en:
Zurara narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "A woman in the state of ihram should 
let her garment hang down to her chest."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 588 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 495  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[   
THE MUHRIM TRIMMING A NAIL OR HAIR

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2690  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم 
عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Abu Basir, who said: I 
asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man in the state of ihram who trimmed one of his nails.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He must give a mudd of food as expiation. If he trims ten nails, he must 
sacrifice a sheep."  
I asked: "What if he trims the nails of both his hands and feet?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he does this in one sitting, he must offer one sacrifice. If he does it in 
two separate sittings, he must offer two sacrifices."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 332 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 
194 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 640 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 162  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2689

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2691  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّطُّولُّ إ َّط ْق َّارَّةُّ إ َّوْ ي َّن ْكَّسِزُّ ن َّعْص ُّهَّا ق َّي ُّو ْذ ِي هِ 
ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّال  ط ُّق ُّزٍ ق َّن ْص َّه ً مِن ْ طَّعَّامٍ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Zurara from Abu Ja'far {a.s}: "Whoever does that (trims nails) 
out of forgetfulness, inattentiveness, or ignorance, there is nothing upon them (no 
penalty)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2690

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a person in the state of 
ihram whose nails grow long or some of them break and cause harm.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They should not cut any of them if they can avoid it. But if they cause 
harm, they may trim them, and for each nail, they must give a handful of food as 
expiation."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 360 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 163 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 9, p. 301  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2691

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2693 - وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim {a.s} about a man who forgot to trim his 
nails before entering the state of ihram until he had already entered it.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should leave them as they are."  
I said: "One of our companions advised him to trim his nails and renew his ihram, so 
he did."  
Imam {a.s} said: "He must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: "If a man plucks his 
underarm hair after entering the state of ihram, he must offer a sacrifice (as 
expiation)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 340 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 
199 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 644 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 161 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 9, p. 301  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
In another narration: "Whoever shaves their head or plucks their underarm 
hair out of forgetfulness, inattentiveness, or ignorance, there is nothing upon them (no 
penalty)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 644 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 160  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm if a person in the state of ihram enters the 
bathhouse, but they should not scrub themselves."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 314 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Vol. 5, p. 386 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 184 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 661 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 537 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 9, p. 238  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2697   -  ومَّز َّ إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّلَّى كَّعْث ِ ن ْن ِ عُّح ْزَّة َّ إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِى ِّ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّحْزِم  وَّ ق َّذْ إ َّكَّ لَّ إَّلْق َّمْلُّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ وَّ 
جَّاج ِن َّي ْهِ وَّ عَّي ْن َّي ْهِ ق َّق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن  َّ إَّلْأ َّمْزَّ ي َّن ْلُّغ ُّ مَّا إ َّرَّى 
ن ُّسُّكاً وَّ جَّلَّق َّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ ن ِق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝  ق َّمَّن ْ كٰان َّ مِن ْكُّمْ مَّزِي ص اً إ َّوْ ن ِهِ إ َّذ ىً مِن ْ رَّإ ْسِهِ ق َّق ِذْي َّه   مِن ْ صِي ٰامٍ إ َّوْ 
صَّذَّق َّه ٍ إ َّوْ ن ُّسُّك ٍ  ۝»ي َّه ُّ إ َّي  َّامٍ وَّ إَّلص َّذَّق َّه ُّ عَّلَّى سِي  َّه ِ مَّسَّاكِي ن َّ لِكُّلِّ مِسْكِي ن ٍ صَّاع  مِن ْ ي َّمْزٍ وَّ رُّوِى 
مِن ْ ي َّمْزٍ وَّ إَّلن  ُّسُّك ُّ س َّاة   لأَّ ي ُّطْعَّمُّ مِن ْهَّا إ َّجَّذ  إ ِلأ َّ إَّلْمَّسَّاكِي ن
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "A person in the state of ihram should not cut the hair of one 
who is not in the state of ihram."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 643 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 515  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2698  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : إ َّ رَّإ َّي ْث َّ إ ِن ْ وَّج َّذْب ُّ عَّلَّى  َّ ق ُّزَّإذ إً إ َّوْ جَّلَّمَّه ً إ َّطْزَّجُّهَّا 
عَّن ِّى  وَّ إ َّن َّا مُّحْزِم  ق َّال اهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
The Prophet {saws} passed by Ka'b ibn Ujrah al-Ansari while he was in the state 
of ihram, and lice had afflicted his head, eyebrows, and eyes. The Messenger of Allah 
{SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said:  
"I did not think the matter would reach the state that I now see."  
He {saws} then commanded him to perform an expiation on his behalf, and Ka'b shaved 
his head, following the words of Allah {SWT}, the Exalted: "But if any of you is ill or has an 
ailment in his head, [making shaving necessary], then [his ransom is] fasting, or 
charity, or sacrifice" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:196).  
The expiation is:  
- Fasting for three days.  
- Giving charity to six needy individuals, with each person receiving a measure (sa') 
of dates - or, as narrated in another report, a mudd of dates.  
- Sacrificing a sheep, which must only be given to the needy and not eaten by 
anyone else.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 656 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 167 
• Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 187 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Part 2, p. 278  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2697

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah {a.s}: "What do you say if I find a tick or 
a louse on me while I am in the state of ihram? Should I remove it?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, even the small ones of these, as they have ascended to a place 
where they do not belong."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2698

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٦٩٨

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked  Imam {a.s} about a person in the state of ihram 
who scratches their head, causing one or two lice to fall off.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no penalty upon them, nor do they need to replace the lice."  
He asked: "How should the person in ihram scratch?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "With their nails, as long as they do not cause bleeding or cut any 
hair."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2699
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٦٩٩

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2701  -  وَّ ق ِى  ج َّن َّزٍ إ ج َّز   وإلأ ولى إ ن  لأ ي حك  إلمحزم رإ سه إ لأ جكا رق ي ق ا ن ا طزإف   
إلأ صان غ.
hadiths.en:
He was asked about a person in the state of ihram who fiddles with their 
beard, causing one or two hairs to fall out.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They must give some food as expiation."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 647  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2700
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧٠٠

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2702  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ هِس َّامِ ن ْن ِ سَّالِمٍ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم 
لِحْي َّي ِهِ وَّ هُّوَّ مُّحْزِم  ق َّسَّق َّظَّ س َّى ْء  مِن َّ إَّلس  َّعْزِ ق َّلْي َّي َّصَّذ َّق ْ ن ِكَّف ٍّ مِن ْ كَّعْك ٍ إ َّوْ سَّوِي ق ٍ
hadiths.en:
In another narration: "A mudd of food or two handfuls."   
It is preferable that the person in the state of ihram does not scratch their head except 
gently, using the tips of their fingers.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2701

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2703  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْح َّارُّوذِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلَّ رَّج ُّل  إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّي َّلَّ ق َّمْلَّه ً وَّ هُّوَّ مُّحْزِم  
ق َّال
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Hisham ibn Salim, Abu Abdullah {a.s} said: "If one of you 
places their hand on their head or beard while in the state of ihram and some hair falls 
out, they should give in charity a handful of biscuits or barley flour."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 361 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 338 
• Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 198 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 645 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 171  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2702
